lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Dubuque Regional Airport; Delta ended MSP service July 2009
Search result: Ozark Airlines second operated 2000-2001 to Dallas/Fort Worth and Chicago-Midway

note: DBQ lost all service Sep 2022 when American Eagle ended ORD flights; brief Denver Air Connection service Nov 2024-Jan 2026 ended by city council vote

lostDestinations.source: Fort Dodge city airport info: Mesaba operated as Delta Connection nonstop to MSP; service ended ~2010 when Fort Dodge retained only EAS to ORD

note: FOD currently EAS-subsidized to ORD via SkyWest/United Express through Mar 2027; MSP service ended when Delta wound down regional turboprop connections

lostDestinations.source: Mason City airport search results: Mesaba EAS flights MSP-Mason City-Fort Dodge began Dec 1982; Delta Connection nonstop to MSP ended June 2010
Search result: Mesaba initiated EAS flights MSP-Mason City-Fort Dodge continuing to Omaha Dec 1982, suspended when Mesaba transitioned to larger aircraft in 1986

note: MCW currently EAS to ORD via SkyWest/United Express since Mar 2021; prior carrier was Air Choice One then Great Lakes

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Sioux Gateway Airport; Delta made final flights into Sioux Gateway April 2012 after Northwest Airlink MSP service

note: SUX lost MSP service Apr 2012 when Delta withdrew; Frontier briefly served DEN 2007-~2010; currently EAS to ORD and DEN via United/SkyWest

lostDestinations.source: Search results: Trans World Express operated JLN-STL 1985-2001
Search results: USAir Express operated JLN-MCI 1991-1992; also Braniff Express 1988-1989
Search results: Ozark Airlines second operated 2000-2001 to DFW and Chicago Midway with one stop at Columbia MO

note: JLN lost DFW service May 2021 when American Eagle withdrew; now served by United Express to ORD and DEN

lostDestinations.source: Search results: Cape Air served CGI-STL 2009-2017; prior Trans World Express (Trans States) also flew STL
Search results: Ozark DC-3s flew STL-CGI-Cairo-Memphis route from 1950; Northwest Airlines still serving CGI in 1986 alongside Brit Airlines
Search results: Contour Airlines serviced CGI-BNA Oct 2022-Sep 2024

note: CGI currently EAS with Contour to ORD and DFW; seasonal PNS service May-Sep

lostDestinations.source: Jefferson City News-Tribune Nov 2019: BACA Airlines 1949-54 served KC; Ozark 1954-1968 served KC; Trans Mo last operator until 1986
Jefferson City News-Tribune: Ozark DC-3s/Martin 404 served JEF with schedules incl Jefferson City, Columbia, STL, Springfield, KC until Columbia Regional opened 1968

note: JEF lost commercial service ~1986 when Trans-Mo shut down; Ozark had earlier served 1954-1968; airport is now general aviation only

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Waterloo Regional Airport; 1989 turboprops flew nonstop to ORD and MDW
Wikipedia: Waterloo Regional Airport; TWA mainline ended 1988, Trans World Express continued until mid-1990s
Wikipedia: Waterloo Regional Airport; 1989 turboprops flew to Kansas City
Wikipedia: Waterloo Regional Airport; Delta was last non-EAS carrier; ended service late 2011

detail: Waterloo lost mainline jet service when TWA ended DC-9 service in 1988; Delta ended MSP service late 2011 triggering EAS designation; now EAS to ORD via American Eagle/Air Wisconsin.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Southeast Iowa Regional Airport; Ozark pulled out of Burlington in 1982
Wikipedia: Southeast Iowa Regional Airport; Ozark had served Burlington with routes to Kansas City since 1956

detail: Ozark Air Lines was the last mainline carrier at Burlington, departing 1982; after a service gap the airport re-entered EAS; Contour Airlines began 12 weekly round trips to ORD on July 1, 2025 under EAS.
source: Wikipedia: Southeast Iowa Regional Airport; search results

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Ottumwa Regional Airport; Great Lakes Aviation served from 1985, ending 2001
Wikipedia: Ottumwa Regional Airport; Mississippi Valley Airlines replaced Ozark 1979-1983

detail: Ottumwa had continuous scheduled service from 1947 (Mid-Continent, then Braniff, Ozark through 1979, Mississippi Valley Airlines 1979-1983, Great Lakes 1985-2001); no airline service since 2001.

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Clinton Municipal Airport Iowa; Feb 1985 Official Airline Guide showed 5 weekday departures to ORD via American Central and Great Lakes; service discontinued by late 1980s

detail: Clinton had scheduled airline flights 1951-1988; Braniff served until 1955 then Ozark; by 1985 Great Lakes/American Central operated to ORD; scheduled service discontinued by late 1980s.

lostDestinations.source: Sioux City Journal 2002: Great Lakes pulled commercial air service ~2001; Great Lakes initiated service Oct 1981 with daily flights to Des Moines
Wikipedia: Spencer Municipal Airport; Great Lakes served MSP via Fort Dodge and Mason City connections

detail: Great Lakes Aviation was founded in Spencer, Iowa in 1977 and launched scheduled service from SPW on October 12, 1981; when Great Lakes pulled out ~2001 Spencer lost all commercial air service.
source: Wikipedia: Spencer Municipal Airport; Sioux City Journal 2002

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Kirksville Regional Airport; Cape Air replaced Air Choice One with STL service 2010-2023
Wikipedia: Kirksville Regional Airport; Air Midwest US Airways Express served MCI Nov 2006-May 2008
Wikipedia: Kirksville Regional Airport; Redwing Airways commuter flights to Kansas City until Oct 2001

detail: Kirksville lost STL service in 2023 when Cape Air ended operations; Contour Airlines began ORD service Aug 1, 2023 under EAS, replacing Cape Air's STL route.
source: Wikipedia: Kirksville Regional Airport; Chicago DOA press release Jul 2023

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia: Rosecrans Memorial Airport; Frontier left 1969, three years before KCI opened 30 miles south
Wikipedia: Rosecrans Memorial Airport; Ozark left 1960; Braniff pulled out 1959

detail: St. Joseph lost all commercial airline service in 1969 when Frontier Airlines departed, three years before Kansas City International opened 30 miles to the south, rendering STJ commercially unviable.

lostDestinations.source: FAA/DOT EAS records: Rolla/Vichy VIH had EAS-subsidized service to STL; service ended ~2002 when Air Midwest withdrew

detail: Rolla National Airport (VIH) had EAS-subsidized commuter service; no scheduled commercial airline service operates today; general aviation only.
source: FAA airport data; search results Rolla National Airport

lostDestinations.source: FAA NPIAS records; Poplar Bluff had limited commuter service to STL in early 1980s

detail: Poplar Bluff Regional Business Airport lost all scheduled commercial service by mid-1980s; currently general aviation only with ~15,000 operations annually and a 5,000 ft runway.
